picks.title: Guns N' Roses and Mammoth
picks.blurb: The legendary rock band headlines Amsterdam's premier arena this Saturday night.

picks.title: Solstice Festival at Ruigoord
picks.blurb: Summer solstice celebration with music, art, performances and rituals in artist community.
picks.why: Solstice celebration, music and performative art

picks.title: Amsterdamse Bos Market & Sound Festival
picks.blurb: Beautiful Things Market with stalls, live music at Klankenfestijn. Casual outdoor weekend browse and picnic spot.

picks.title: Sunset Yoga in Rembrandtpark
picks.blurb: Free sunset yoga on summer solstice evening, hosted by David Lloyd Clubs.

picks.title: Music on Sunday: Hortus Botanicus Evening Concerts
picks.blurb: Live evening concerts in botanical gardens on summer solstice.

picks.title: Eye Classics
picks.blurb: Film history on the big screen: classics screened in 35mm whenever possible for cinephiles.

picks.title: World Press Photo Exhibition
picks.blurb: Iconic photojournalism exhibition showcasing the year's most compelling global news and documentary images.

picks.title: Bite of Amsterdam 2026
picks.blurb: Major food festival with 7 reasons foodies are lining up: tastings, demos, and culinary stars across three days.
picks.why: Headline food event of the season
picks.source: Your Little Black Book · I amsterdam · Bite of Amsterdam / I amsterdam · I amsterdam, Your Little Black Book

picks.title: Bacchus Wine Festival
picks.blurb: Wine tasting: 300+ wines globally, cheese, food trucks, DJs, live acts. Dancing till late, non-wine options too.
picks.source: I amsterdam · Bacchus Wine Festival / Amsterdam Spotte · I amsterdam, Amsterdamian

picks.title: Holland Festival
picks.blurb: Amsterdam's premier annual arts festival spans theatre, dance, opera, and multimedia. Final weekend to catch world-class performances.

picks.title: Open Garden Days (Open Tuinen Dagen)
picks.blurb: Explore 30 private hidden gardens behind historic canal houses, normally closed to public.
picks.why: Rare access to private canal gardens, architectural beauty
picks.source: I amsterdam · I amsterdam, Amsterdam Guide

picks.title: The Movies — arthouse cinema
picks.blurb: The Netherlands’ oldest cinema still running — Art Deco rooms, a dinner-and-a-film deal, arthouse bills.
picks.why: Indoor · dinner + a film · perfect when it’s wet

picks.title: Queer Power
picks.blurb: World Pride celebration: Eye's queer cinema festival honouring diverse makers globally, opening this weekend.
picks.why: Opens during Amsterdam Pride; world-class curated programme

picks.title: Jardin Rouge Festival
picks.blurb: Outdoor music festival at artist village with opera lovers and cultural newcomers; 10th anniversary.

picks.title: Yumna Al-Arashi — Body as Resistance
picks.blurb: First solo museum show for Al-Arashi — photography, sculpture and book confront the policing of women’s bodies.

picks.title: Carel Visser — sculpture in the gardens
picks.blurb: Free outdoor sculpture show — the first artist solo in the Rijksmuseum gardens, opening this weekend.

picks.title: East Beach — rooftop city beach
picks.blurb: A new 800m² rooftop city beach above Oosterpark — sand, beach chairs, drinks, open to all.

picks.title: De Pimpelmees
picks.blurb: Cosy neighbourhood wine bar — European wines from €6, Italian snacks, free chips. Opened early 2026.

picks.title: Celeste
picks.blurb: Soul and R&B artist Celeste performs in Paradiso's main hall on Saturday.

picks.title: Jodeci
picks.blurb: R&B legends Jodeci bring 90s soul and smooth vocals to AFAS Live on Saturday.

picks.title: John Summit
picks.blurb: House music producer John Summit spins at the outdoor Lofi Courtyard on Sunday.

picks.title: Bloodywood
picks.blurb: Delhi-based metal band Bloodywood brings high-energy fusion sound to Melkweg Max.

picks.title: GRIMM
picks.blurb: Hip-hop and ballet collide in a wild reimagining of classic fairy tales for families aged 8+.
picks.why: Spectacularly inventive dance show, highly recommended

picks.title: Havenfestival 2026
picks.blurb: Bustling harbour activities and demonstrations for all ages across Java Island's waterfront.
picks.why: Free, family-friendly festival with hands-on maritime activi

picks.title: 24 uur Nieuw-West
picks.blurb: District-wide day of free activities: street art tours, DJ sets, brewery tastings, bird watching, sports, creativity.

picks.title: Museum Market
picks.blurb: Sunday market with arts, crafts, handmade goods, food vendors and live music.
picks.why: Free entry, arts and local makers

picks.title: Spinoza — De Mokum Musical
picks.blurb: Open-air musical about philosopher Spinoza set in old Amsterdam, in the forest theatre.
picks.why: Cross-source (LBB + I amsterdam) · open-air · dry-night plan

picks.title: Eye(s) Open: New Perspectives on Colonial Film Heritage
picks.blurb: Eleven artists reimagine Eye's colonial-era Indonesian and Surinamese films with ten new commissioned works.
picks.why: Major exhibition, artists respond to historical archive

picks.title: American Identities
picks.blurb: Levinthal’s staged photos and ChicanX prints pick apart the myths and power plays of American identity.

picks.title: Danh Vo at the Stedelijk
picks.blurb: Danh Vo weaves objects, images and text into a quiet meditation on identity and human ties.

picks.title: Zaanse Schans windmills
picks.blurb: Eight working windmills, cheese and crafts on the Zaan — free grounds, ~17 min train from Centraal.
picks.why: Short train · outdoor · classic kid day

picks.title: Haarlem — the easy escape
picks.blurb: A calmer historic city 15 min away — Grote Markt, the St-Bavo organ, a Saturday market and café-lined square.

picks.title: Foodhallen
picks.blurb: An indoor food hall in a converted tram depot — 20-odd stalls, long tables and two bars under one roof.
picks.why: Indoor · grazing for a crowd · weather-proof

picks.title: Moeders — Dutch home cooking
picks.blurb: Walls papered with strangers’ mothers’ photos and a stamppot-and-stew menu built for a grey evening.
picks.why: Snug · classic Dutch · ideal in the rain

picks.title: Café de Klos — ribs & grill
picks.blurb: A tiny, packed grill house off the canals famous for sticky spare ribs — no frills, no reservations.
picks.why: Warm · cheap · duck in out of the wet

picks.title: Wynand Fockink — jenever tasting
picks.blurb: A 1679 tasting room behind Dam Square pouring its own jenevers and liqueurs — you bend to the brimful glass.
picks.why: Historic · indoor · made for a downpour

picks.title: Café Chris — oldest brown café
picks.blurb: Open since 1624: a creaky, golden-lit Jordaan brown café for a slow afternoon beer while it pours outside.

picks.title: Eye Filmmuseum — repertory screening
picks.blurb: Across the IJ by free ferry: a striking film museum with a daily repertory programme and a river-view café.
picks.why: Indoor · a film + the ferry ride · weather-proof

picks.title: Sauna Deco — Art Deco baths
picks.blurb: A 1920s Art Deco bathhouse (salvaged from a Paris department store) with saunas, steam and a quiet tea room.
picks.why: Warm · restorative · the ultimate cold-and-wet antidote

picks.title: Pllek — waterfront hang
picks.blurb: A cargo-container café-bar on the NDSM wharf — big windows onto the IJ, sofas, and a wood stove when it turns.
picks.why: Indoor seating · IJ views · cozy when grey

picks.title: Tony’s Chocolonely Super Store
picks.blurb: The flagship of the slavery-free chocolate brand — make-your-own bars, a café, and a serious sweet-tooth detour.
picks.why: Indoor · kid-friendly · a happy rainy hour

picks.title: ARTIS Royal Zoo
picks.blurb: The country’s oldest zoo — big cats, an aquarium and a planetarium across a leafy 19th-century park.
picks.why: All-day with kids · indoor + outdoor mix

picks.title: NEMO Science Museum
picks.blurb: Five floors of hands-on experiments in the big green ship — plus a free rooftop with city views.

picks.title: Vondelpark playgrounds
picks.blurb: Amsterdam’s favourite park: open-air playgrounds, the Kinderkookkafé, ducks and room to run.
picks.why: Free · easy any time with kids

picks.title: Goat Farm Ridammerhoeve
picks.blurb: A working organic goat farm in the forest — bottle-feed the kids (the goat kind), then pancakes on the terrace.

picks.title: TunFun indoor playground
picks.blurb: A giant indoor adventure playground hidden in a former traffic underpass — slides, ball pits, trampolines.

picks.title: Madurodam
picks.blurb: The whole of the Netherlands at 1:25 — miniature canals, Schiphol and windmills kids can tower over.

picks.title: Efteling
picks.blurb: Europe’s oldest fairytale theme park — enchanted forest, dark rides and proper coasters in a storybook world.

picks.title: As
picks.blurb: Wood-fired, ingredient-led cooking and natural wine in a striking round garden pavilion.

picks.title: BAK
picks.blurb: A light-filled warehouse with an open kitchen and an art-covered wall; a set seasonal menu of produce-driven dishes.

picks.title: Brut de Mer
picks.blurb: Oysters, fish and bubbles — a tiny, lively seafood bar in De Pijp.

picks.title: The Frozen Fountain
picks.blurb: The country’s best-known design store — floors of furniture, lighting and covetable objects to browse.

picks.title: Brewery Homeland
picks.blurb: A craft brewery and beach bar on the IJ — tank-fresh beer and a waterfront sun-trap terrace.

picks.title: Noorderlicht
picks.blurb: A greenhouse café on the NDSM wharf — string lights, salvaged charm and a big waterside terrace.

picks.title: De Filmhallen
picks.blurb: A nine-screen arthouse cinema in a converted tram depot — with a bustling food hall right next door.
picks.why: Arthouse · in the old tram depot

picks.title: Kriterion
picks.blurb: A legendary student-run cinema and café since 1945 — arthouse programming with a buzzy terrace.

picks.title: Vlieger
picks.blurb: A two-floor temple to paper since 1869 — hundreds of colours, handmade sheets and card stacked to the ceiling.

picks.title: Athenaeum Boekhandel
picks.blurb: The intellectual heart of the Spui — a beloved bookshop and an international newsstand packed floor-to-ceiling.

picks.title: The American Book Center
picks.blurb: A sprawling three-floor English-language bookshop — a spiral of shelves and a basement of zines and comics.

picks.title: Patta
picks.blurb: The homegrown streetwear label and culture hub — drops, sneakers and the community that put Amsterdam on the map.

picks.title: Moise
picks.blurb: An understated De Pijp boutique with a razor-sharp eye — Acne, Lemaire, Marant and the like, hand-picked and beautifully shown.

picks.title: Cowboys to Catwalk
picks.blurb: A treasure-trove of vintage designer — Margiela, Marc Jacobs and one-off finds rummaged from decades of fashion.

picks.title: Mobilia
picks.blurb: Three floors of high design on the Utrechtsestraat — icons by the masters alongside more attainable covetables.
